### Post by jtp51 on 2006-03-18
I followed the guide on SensorInstallHowto at [https://wiki.ubuntu.com/SensorInstallHowto?highlight=%28sensor%29](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/SensorInstallHowto?highlight=%28sensor%29).

When I run:

tpatrick@ip68-13-59-163:/etc$ sensors
No sensors found!


So, I checked my /etc/modules file and I have the following message at the end of the file:

# Generated by sensors-detect on Sat Mar 18 19:13:12 2006
# I2C adapter drivers
i2c-isa
# I2C chip drivers
# no driver for SMSC 47M15x/192 Super IO Fan Sensors yet


Errr... so this means my machine is too new? (That would be a first.)

I'm displaying the message "Setting sensors limits... failed" at start-up. I believe this isn't critical, however clarification would be appreciated.

BTW: This might explain why my CPU fan runs 100% in Ubuntu, but not WindowsXP?

Thanks,
